```text
64	let _audioCtx = null
65	function getCtx() {
66	  if (!_audioCtx || _audioCtx.state === 'closed') {
67	    _audioCtx = new (window.AudioContext || window.webkitAudioContext)()
68	  }
69	  if (_audioCtx.state === 'suspended') _audioCtx.resume()
70	  return _audioCtx
71	}
72	
73	const urlCache = new Map()   // word -> mp3 url | null
74	const bufCache = new Map()   // url  -> AudioBuffer
75	
76	async function fetchUrl(word) {
77	  const key = word.toLowerCase().split(' ')[0]
78	  if (urlCache.has(key)) return urlCache.get(key)
79	  try {
80	    const res = await fetch(`https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/en/${encodeURIComponent(key)}`,
81	      { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000) })
82	    if (!res.ok) throw new Error()
83	    const data = await res.json()
84	    const raw = data[0]?.phonetics?.find(p => p.audio)?.audio || null
85	    const url = raw ? (raw.startsWith('http') ? raw : 'https:' + raw) : null
86	    urlCache.set(key, url); return url
87	  } catch { urlCache.set(key, null); return null }
88	}
89	
90	async function fetchBuffer(url) {
91	  if (bufCache.has(url)) return bufCache.get(url)
92	  try {
93	    const ab = await fetch(url).then(r => r.arrayBuffer())
94	    const buf = await getCtx().decodeAudioData(ab)
95	    bufCache.set(url, buf); return buf
96	  } catch { return null }
97	}
98	
99	let _currentSource = null
100	function stopAudio() {
101	  try { _currentSource?.stop() } catch {}
102	  _currentSource = null
103	}
104	
105	function playBuffer(buffer, startSec = 0, endSec = null) {
106	  stopAudio()
107	  const ctx = getCtx()
108	  const src = ctx.createBufferSource()
109	  src.buffer = buffer
110	  src.connect(ctx.destination)
111	  const dur = endSec != null ? endSec - startSec : buffer.duration - startSec
112	  src.start(0, startSec, dur)
113	  _currentSource = src
114	  return src
115	}
116	
117	// ── TTS fallback ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118	
119	function speak(text, rate = 0.82) {
120	  window.speechSynthesis?.cancel()
121	  const u = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(text)
122	  u.lang = 'en-US'; u.rate = rate
123	  window.speechSynthesis?.speak(u)
124	}
125	
126	// ── useWordAudio hook ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	function useWordAudio(word, ipa) {
129	  const syllables = useMemo(() => syllabify(word), [word])
130	  const realSyls  = useMemo(() => syllables.filter(s => !s.isSep), [syllables])
131	  const [activeSylIdx, setActiveSylIdx] = useState(-1)
132	  const [loading, setLoading]           = useState(false)
133	  const timersRef = useRef([])
134	
135	  function clearTimers() { timersRef.current.forEach(clearTimeout); timersRef.current = [] }
136	  function reset() { clearTimers(); stopAudio(); setActiveSylIdx(-1) }
137	
138	  // Determine syllable count: prefer IPA count, fallback to orthographic
139	  const sylCount = useMemo(() => countIPASyllables(ipa) ?? realSyls.length, [ipa, realSyls])
140	
141	  async function getBuffer() {
142	    const url = await fetchUrl(word)
143	    return url ? fetchBuffer(url) : null
144	  }
145	
146	  function scheduleHighlight(durationMs) {
147	    const n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)
148	    const perSyl = durationMs / n
149	    realSyls.slice(0, n).forEach((s, i) => {
150	      timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(s.idx), i * perSyl))
151	    })
152	    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), durationMs + 400))
153	  }
154	
155	  // 🔊 Play full word + sequential syllable highlight
156	  async function playFull() {
157	    reset()
158	    setLoading(true)
159	    const buf = await getBuffer()
160	    setLoading(false)
161	    if (buf) {
162	      scheduleHighlight(buf.duration * 1000)
163	      playBuffer(buf)
164	    } else {
165	      speak(word, 0.5)
166	      scheduleHighlight(realSyls.length * 420)
167	    }
168	  }
169	
170	  // Click syllable → play that audio slice (proportional)
171	  async function playSyl(sylIdx) {
172	    reset()
173	    setActiveSylIdx(sylIdx)
174	    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), 1500))
175	
176	    const buf = await getBuffer()
177	    const n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)
178	
179	    if (buf && n > 1) {
180	      const realIdx = realSyls.findIndex(s => s.idx === sylIdx)
181	      if (realIdx >= 0) {
182	        const perSyl = buf.duration / n
183	        playBuffer(buf, realIdx * perSyl, (realIdx + 1) * perSyl)
184	        return
185	      }
186	    }
187	    if (buf) { playBuffer(buf); return }
188	    // last resort TTS for this syllable
189	    const sylText = realSyls.find(s => s.idx === sylIdx)?.text || word
190	    speak(sylText, 0.65)
191	  }
192	
193	  useEffect(() => { reset(); return reset }, [word])
194	  return { syllables, activeSylIdx, loading, playFull, playSyl }
195	}
196	
197	// ── SyllableWord ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
198	
199	const SYL_COLORS = [
200	  { base: 'text-blue-300',   active: 'text-blue-100'   },
201	  { base: 'text-orange-300', active: 'text-orange-100' },
202	  { base: 'text-emerald-300',active: 'text-emerald-100'},
203	  { base: 'text-pink-300',   active: 'text-pink-100'   },
204	]
205	
206	function SyllableWord({ syllables, activeSylIdx, onSylClick, size = 'text-4xl' }) {
207	  return (
208	    <span className={`${size} font-bold tracking-wide select-none`}>
209	      {syllables.map((s, i) => {
210	        if (s.isSep) return <span key={i} className="text-gray-500">{s.text}</span>
211	        const col = SYL_COLORS[s.idx % SYL_COLORS.length]
212	        const active = activeSylIdx === s.idx
213	        return (
214	          <span key={i} onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); onSylClick(s.idx) }}
215	            className={`cursor-pointer inline-block transition-all duration-150
216	              ${active ? `${col.active} scale-110 underline underline-offset-4 drop-shadow-[0_0_10px_currentColor]`
217	                       : `${col.base} hover:opacity-75`}`}>
218	            {s.text}
219	          </span>
220	        )
221	      })}
222	    </span>
223	  )
224	}
225

227	
228	function loadProgress() { try { return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('vocab_progress') || '{}') } catch { return {} } }
229	function saveProgress(bookName, unitIdx, wordIdx, val) {
230	  try { const s = loadProgress(); s[`vocab_${bookName}_${unitIdx}_${wordIdx}`] = val; localStorage.setItem('vocab_progress', JSON.stringify(s)) } catch {}
231	}
232	function shuffle(arr) {
233	  const a = [...arr]
234	  for (let i = a.length - 1; i > 0; i--) { const j = Math.floor(Math.random() * (i + 1));[a[i], a[j]] = [a[j], a[i]] }
235	  return a
236	}
237	function makeBlank(w) {
238	  if (w.includes(' ')) return w.split(' ').map(p => p.length <= 2 ? p : p[0] + '_'.repeat(p.length - 2) + p[p.length - 1]).join(' ')
239	  return w.length <= 2 ? w : w[0] + '_'.repeat(w.length - 2) + w[w.length - 1]
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 01:17:13Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The `playSyl` function at line 171 seems correct in theory. It:
1. Gets the audio buffer
2. Calculates the syllable index
3. Slices the buffer proportionally

But I see a potential issue:

At line 179: `if (buf && n > 1)` - this means if the word has only 1 syllable (n=1), it falls through to `if (buf) { playBuffer(buf); return }` which plays the whole audio. That's correct.

For multi-syllable words, it calculates `perSyl = buf.duration / n` and plays from `realIdx * perSyl` to `(realIdx + 1) * perSyl`.


The real problem might be a mismatch between how syllables are counted via IPA versus how they're actually represented in the orthographic data. The `fetchUrl` function extracts just the first word from compound words by splitting on spaces, which could be losing information about the full word's syllable structure.

More importantly, when the dictionary API doesn't return audio, the code falls back to TTS, but the user is reporting that clicking individual syllables plays the entire word instead of just that syllable. 242	// ── Recording hook ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```text
243	
244	function useRecorder() {
245	  const [recState, setRecState] = useState('idle')
246	  const [recUrl, setRecUrl]     = useState(null)
247	  const mrRef = useRef(null); const chunksRef = useRef([]); const timerRef = useRef(null)
248	
249	  function reset() {
250	    setRecState('idle')
251	    setRecUrl(prev => { if (prev) URL.revokeObjectURL(prev); return null })
252	    mrRef.current?.stream?.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())
253	  }
254	  async function startRec() {
255	    try {
256	      reset(); stopAudio(); window.speechSynthesis?.cancel()
257	      const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true })
258	      const mr = new MediaRecorder(stream); mrRef.current = mr; chunksRef.current = []
259	      mr.ondataavailable = e => { if (e.data.size > 0) chunksRef.current.push(e.data) }
260	      mr.onstop = () => { setRecUrl(URL.createObjectURL(new Blob(chunksRef.current, { type: 'audio/webm' }))); setRecState('recorded'); stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop()) }
261	      mr.start(); setRecState('recording')
262	      timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => mr.state === 'recording' && mr.stop(), 4000)
263	    } catch { setRecState('idle'); alert('需要麦克风权限') }
264	  }
265	  function stopRec() { clearTimeout(timerRef.current); if (mrRef.current?.state === 'recording') mrRef.current.stop() }
266	  function playRec() { if (recUrl) { stopAudio(); window.speechSynthesis?.cancel(); new Audio(recUrl).play() } }
267	  useEffect(() => () => { clearTimeout(timerRef.current); reset() }, [])
268	  return { recState, recUrl, startRec, stopRec, playRec, reset }
269	}

312	              </div>

314	            </button>
315	          )
316	        })}
317	      </div>

361	        <button onClick={onBack} className="text-gray-400 hover:text-white text-sm">← 返回</button>
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about a conceptual question: if we mark dots between syllables first, would that make audio splitting easier? Let me think about this carefully.

The user might be thinking: if we visually mark syllables with dots (like "teach·er"), then we know exactly where syllables are, and can use that to cut the audio. But the question is whether this helps with the actual audio splitting.

Let me explain the fundamental issue:

1. **Visual syllable marking (orthographic)** - marking dots between syllables like "teach·er" or "com·put·er" is purely a text operation. We can do this from the written word.


2. **Audio splitting** - the actual problem is finding where each syllable begins and ends in time within the audio file. Text markings tell us which letters belong to which syllable, but they don't tell us the temporal boundaries in the audio—those depend entirely on how the speaker pronounces the word, not on the orthography.

Our current approach of dividing the audio proportionally by syllable count is a reasonable approximation, but getting exact timestamps would require forced alignment tools like Montreal Forced Aligner or speech-to-text APIs that provide phoneme-level timing. The dots in the text are useful for knowing how many segments to create and what to display, but they don't actually solve the audio timing problem—that information has to come from analyzing the audio itself.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 01:21:14Z)**_

这是个很好的问题，直接讲清楚：

## 点标注 vs 音频切分——两件完全不同的事

**点标注（文字层面）**：
```
teach·er    com·pu·ter    break·fast
```
这是告诉我们：这个词有几个音节、哪些字母属于哪个音节。**纯文字操作，不需要音频。**

---

**音频切分（时间轴层面）**：
```
音频波形:  [████████████████████████████████]
           0ms                            450ms
问题：     "teach" 在哪里结束？"er" 从哪里开始？
```
这是一个**时间定位**问题。点标注完全帮不上忙——因为它没有时间信息。

---

## 我们现在的方案（等比例切分）

```
teach·er → 2个音节 → 各占 50%
音频时长 450ms → [0~225ms = teach] [225~450ms = er]
```

问题在于：真实发音里 "teach" 可能占 65%，"er" 只占 35%。比例是**猜的**，不是测量出来的。

---

## 为什么标注了点也不能精确切分

标注 `teach·er` 只是确认了"有2个音节"，但这个信息**我们已经有了**（从IPA数音节）。  
真正需要的是：**每个音节在时间轴上的精确起止点**，这需要：

- **强制对齐（Forced Alignment）**：把音频和音素对齐，计算量大
- **语音识别API的音素时间戳**：Azure/Google，需要付费
- **这就是上次讨论的MFA方案**，精确但复杂

---

## 结论

| 方案 | 能做什么 | 解决切分吗 |
|------|---------|----------|
| 文字加点标注 | 显示音节、确认数量 | ❌ 不解决 |
| IPA数音节（现有）| 知道切几份 | 部分解决（等比例近似）|
| 强制对齐/MFA | 精确时间戳 | ✅ 完全解决 |

**你的理解误区**：以为标了点就知道音频在哪里切——但文字和音频是两个维度，标点不带时间信息。

---

目前等比例方案对大多数词已经够用（误差在一个音节的边界处），如果想追求精准，就需要MFA方案。要继续推进哪个方向？

---

But the reality is that most of these are local tools, not web scraping targets. The core issue is that phoneme timing information lives inside the audio waveform itself, not in the HTML we can scrape.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 01:23:18Z)**_

**Scrapling 是 Python 的 HTML 抓取库**——问题不在抓取工具，而在于：**没有哪个免费网站把音素时间戳放在 HTML 里供你抓**。

## 为什么抓不到

音素时间戳不是网页数据，它存在于**音频波形内部**：

```
网页能抓到的：        抓不到的：
- IPA 文字 /ˈtiːtʃər/ ✓    - t 在 0ms~40ms ✗
- MP3 文件 URL ✓            - iː 在 40ms~180ms ✗
- 词义/例句 ✓               - tʃ 在 180ms~280ms ✗
```

想拿到时间戳，必须**分析音频本身**，不是抓网页。

---

## 真正可行的免费方案

| 方案 | 原理 | 可行性 |
|------|------|--------|
| **eSpeak-NG WASM** | 开源TTS，可输出每个音素的时间戳，在浏览器里运行 | ✅ 免费，但音色是合成的 |
| **Gentle**（本地服务）| 强制对齐，把真实MP3和IPA对齐 | ✅ 免费，需本地服务器 |
| **WebAssembly whisper** | 语音识别反推时间戳 | 🟡 太重，浏览器跑不动 |
| **现有等比例方案** | IPA数音节，等分时间 | ✅ 现成可用，有误差 |

---

## 我的建议

当前场景是**小学词汇**（最多3-4音节的短词），等比例误差不大。真正的问题是：

> 音节点击时用户能否感受到区别？

比如 `com·pu·ter`：
- 第1音节：0~33% → 听到 "com"
- 第2音节：33~66% → 听到 "pyu"  
- 第3音节：66~100% → 听到 "ter"

这已经能区分，只是边界不精确。

```text
Based on the search results, here's what I found about espeak-ng with WASM/npm in the browser context:

## espeak-ng WASM in the Browser

eSpeak-NG speech synthesizer is compiled to JavaScript via Emscripten, making it available as an npm package for browser use. Browser-based implementations provide no servers or APIs, offering pure JavaScript functionality.

## NPM Packages

The npm espeak-ng package provides eSpeak-NG compiled to JavaScript via Emscripten. Additionally, text2wav.node.js compiles espeak-ng into Javascript and WebAssembly libraries using Emscripten and includes compiled voice data from espeak-ng.

## Phoneme and Events Support

eSpeak NG converts text to phonemes with pitch and length information. Browser implementations support accessing phoneme events through callbacks, as shown in code examples where synthesize methods receive callbacks with samples and events.

## 2024 Updates

While the search results reference recent activity, specific details about 2024 feature releases focused on "phoneme events timing" were not prominently featured in the results. However, there was a contribution to add stress marks to phoneme events mentioned in the release discussion, indicating ongoing development of phoneme-related features in the espeak-ng project.
```

```text
Here are the search results for espeakng.js with information about browser, npm, phoneme callbacks, and audio samples:

## Key Findings

**espeakng.js Overview:**
espeakng.js allows client-side text-to-speech synthesis in any browser supporting Web workers and the Web Audio API.

**Phoneme and Audio Callback Support:**
The project includes callback functionality for working with phonemes and audio samples. The API includes a `synthesize(text, cb(samples, events))` function that synthesizes text and calls a callback function when done, passing the generated audio samples and events.

**Browser Initialization:**
You create a new eSpeakNG instance with the path of the worker as the first argument and can supply a callback function that will be called once the worker is ready.

**Related NPM Packages:**
- espeak-ng is an eSpeak-NG speech synthesizer compiled to JavaScript + WASM
- @echogarden/espeak-ng-emscripten is an eSpeak-NG speech synthesizer compiled to JavaScript via Emscripten

**CDN Availability:**
CDN files are available including espeakng.min.js, espeakng.worker.js, and espeakng.worker.data for latest and specific versions.

The main repositories are available on GitHub at `espeak-ng/espeak-ng` (main project) and `pettarin/espeakng.js-cdn` (CDN distribution).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.
```

<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Check espeakng.min.js API

`cat /Users/hong/english-input-app/public/espeak/espeakng.min.js`

```text
/*
 * Copyright (C) 2014-2017 Eitan Isaacson
 *
 * This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify
 * it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by
 * the Free Software Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or
 * (at your option) any later version.
 *
 * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
 * but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
 * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
 * GNU General Public License for more details.
 *
 * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
 * along with this program; if not, see: <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.
 */

function eSpeakNG(worker_path, ready_cb) {
  this.worker = new Worker(worker_path);
  this.ready = false;
  this.worker.onmessage = function(e) {
    if (e.data !== 'ready') {
      return;
    }
    this.worker.onmessage = null;
    this.worker.addEventListener('message', this);
    this.ready = true;
    if (ready_cb) {
      ready_cb();
    }
  }.bind(this);
}

eSpeakNG.prototype.handleEvent = function (evt) {
  var callback = evt.data.callback;
  if (callback && this[callback]) {
    this[callback].apply(this, evt.data.result);
    if (evt.data.done) {
      delete this[callback];
    }
    return;
  }
};

function _createAsyncMethod(method) {
  return function() {
    var lastArg = arguments[arguments.length - 1];
    var message = { method: method, args: Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments, 0) };
    if (typeof lastArg == 'function') {
      var callback = '_' + method + '_' + Math.random().toString().substring(2) +'_cb';
      this[callback] = lastArg;
      message.args.pop();
      message.callback = callback;
    }
    this.worker.postMessage(message);
  };
}

for (var method of [
    'list_voices',
    'get_rate',
    'get_pitch',
    'set_rate',
    'set_pitch',
    'set_voice',
    'synthesize'
    ]) {
  eSpeakNG.prototype[method] = _createAsyncMethod(method);
}
```
</details></tool-use>

## Cursor 指令

**任务：修改 `/Users/hong/english-input-app/src/components/VocabStudy.jsx`**

### 改动 1：eSpeak 函数（加在 `speak` 函数后面，约第125行）

在 `function speak(...)` 之后插入：

```javascript
// ── eSpeak-NG (precise syllable boundaries) ───────────────────────────────────

let _espeakInst = null
const espeakSylCache = new Map() // word → { buffer: AudioBuffer, bounds: number[] }

function loadEspeakNG() {
  if (_espeakInst) return Promise.resolve(_espeakInst)
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    if (document.getElementById('espeakng-script')) {
      // script already injected, wait for global
      const poll = setInterval(() => {
        if (window.eSpeakNG) { clearInterval(poll); initInst(resolve) }
      }, 100)
      return
    }
    const s = document.createElement('script')
    s.id = 'espeakng-script'
    s.src = '/espeak/espeakng.min.js'
    s.onload = () => initInst(resolve)
    s.onerror = () => resolve(null)
    document.head.appendChild(s)
  })
}

function initInst(resolve) {
  try {
    const inst = new window.eSpeakNG('/espeak/espeakng.worker.js', () => {
      _espeakInst = inst
      inst.set_voice('en', () => resolve(inst))
    })
  } catch { resolve(null) }
}

// English vowel phonemes in eSpeak notation
const ES_VOWELS = new Set([
  'I','E','{','V','Q','U','@','i:','u:','O:','3:',
  'eI','aI','QI','@U','aU','I@','E@','U@','a','e','i','o','u'
])

async function getEspeakSyl(word) {
  if (espeakSylCache.has(word)) return espeakSylCache.get(word)
  const inst = await loadEspeakNG()
  if (!inst) return null
  return new Promise((resolve) => {
    const timeout = setTimeout(() => resolve(null), 4000)
    inst.synthesize(word, (samples, events) => {
      clearTimeout(timeout)
      try {
        // Build AudioBuffer from Int16 samples (eSpeak rate: 22050)
        const RATE = 22050
        const ctx = getCtx()
        const abuf = ctx.createBuffer(1, samples.length, RATE)
        const ch = abuf.getChannelData(0)
        for (let i = 0; i < samples.length; i++) ch[i] = samples[i] / 32768
        // Find vowel phoneme events
        const vowels = (events || [])
          .filter(e => e.type === 7 && ES_VOWELS.has(e.id))
          .sort((a, b) => a.audio_position - b.audio_position)
        // Compute syllable boundaries in seconds
        const total = samples.length / RATE
        let bounds = [0, total] // default: 1 syllable
        if (vowels.length >= 2) {
          bounds = [0]
          for (let i = 1; i < vowels.length; i++) {
            const prevEnd = (vowels[i - 1].audio_position + (vowels[i - 1].length || 0)) / 1000
            const nextStart = vowels[i].audio_position / 1000
            bounds.push((prevEnd + nextStart) / 2)
          }
          bounds.push(total)
        }
        const result = { buffer: abuf, bounds }
        espeakSylCache.set(word, result)
        resolve(result)
      } catch { resolve(null) }
    })
  })
}
```

---

### 改动 2：`playSyl` 函数（在 `useWordAudio` 内，约第171行）

将整个 `playSyl` 函数替换为：

```javascript
  async function playSyl(sylIdx) {
    reset()
    setActiveSylIdx(sylIdx)
    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), 1500))

    // 1. Try eSpeak for precise syllable audio
    const es = await getEspeakSyl(word)
    if (es && es.bounds.length > 2) {
      // bounds has N+1 entries for N syllables
      const realIdx = realSyls.findIndex(s => s.idx === sylIdx)
      if (realIdx >= 0 && realIdx < es.bounds.length - 1) {
        playBuffer(es.buffer, es.bounds[realIdx], es.bounds[realIdx + 1])
        return
      }
    }

    // 2. Fallback: real audio with equal-proportion slice
    const buf = await getBuffer()
    const n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)
    if (buf && n > 1) {
      const realIdx = realSyls.findIndex(s => s.idx === sylIdx)
      if (realIdx >= 0) {
        const perSyl = buf.duration / n
        playBuffer(buf, realIdx * perSyl, (realIdx + 1) * perSyl)
        return
      }
    }
    if (buf) { playBuffer(buf); return }
    speak(realSyls.find(s => s.idx === sylIdx)?.text || word, 0.65)
  }
```

---

### 改动 3：`SyllableWord` 组件（约第206行）

将整个 `SyllableWord` 函数替换为：

```javascript
function SyllableWord({ syllables, activeSylIdx, onSylClick, size = 'text-4xl' }) {
  return (
    <span className={`${size} font-bold tracking-wide select-none`}>
      {syllables.map((s, i) => {
        if (s.isSep) return <span key={i} className="text-gray-500">{s.text}</span>
        const col = SYL_COLORS[s.idx % SYL_COLORS.length]
        const active = activeSylIdx === s.idx
        const prevIsRealSyl = i > 0 && !syllables[i - 1].isSep
        return (
          <span key={i} className="inline-flex items-center">
            {prevIsRealSyl && (
              <span className="text-gray-600 select-none" style={{ fontSize: '0.45em', margin: '0 0.15em' }}>·</span>
            )}
            <span onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); onSylClick(s.idx) }}
              className={`cursor-pointer inline-block transition-all duration-150
                ${active
                  ? `${col.active} scale-110 underline underline-offset-4 drop-shadow-[0_0_10px_currentColor]`
                  : `${col.base} hover:opacity-75`}`}>
              {s.text}
            </span>
          </span>
        )
      })}
    </span>
  )
}
```

---

---

**现在需要你在浏览器里测一下**：打开 http://localhost:8765 → 单词 → 三年级下册 → 任意单元 → 点一个多音节词（比如 teacher、eraser）的某个音节色块。

**可能遇到的问题：**

| 现象 | 原因 | 修复 |
|------|------|------|
| 控制台报 `e.id undefined` | phoneme字段名不同 | 把 `ES_VOWELS.has(e.id)` 改成 `ES_VOWELS.has(e.phoneme)` |

| eSpeak加载失败（网络） | worker路径问题 | 检查 `/espeak/espeakng.worker.js` 返回200 |

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 05:15:08Z)**_

## 问题剖析

### 问题1：点号对齐

原因：每个音节被包在独立的 `inline-flex items-center` 里，造成各自独立的 flex 对齐基准，大小不同时高低不一。

**修复**：改用 `items-baseline`（文字基线对齐），把整个词放进一个统一的 flex 容器。

---

### 问题2：about 切成 ab | out 而不是 a | bout

**根本原因**：当前边界用的是**相邻元音中点**公式：

```
ə 结束(130ms) ←── 'b' 占据 ──→ aʊ 开始(200ms)
                 boundary = (130+200)/2 = 165ms  ← 切到b的中间
```

'b' 是第二音节的起始辅音（onset），但中点公式把它劈开了。

**根本规则**：英语音节结构 = **onset + vowel + coda**。两个元音之间的辅音优先归属下一个音节（Maximal Onset Principle），所以 a·bout，而不是 ab·out。

**改进方案**：边界设在**前一元音结束处**，而不是中点：

```
ə 开始→结束(130ms) | b + aʊ + t
边界 = 130ms  → "a" | "bout" ✓
```

这在语言学上更正确，eSpeak 的 `audio_position + length` 就是元音结束时间。

**能否完全解决**：大多数情况是的，但对于 `teaching`（tʃ 是 teach 的韵尾）等复杂案例仍可能有轻微偏差。对小学词汇来说已经足够准确。

---

## 两处修复代码（交给 Cursor）

### 修复1：SyllableWord 对齐

```jsx
function SyllableWord({ syllables, activeSylIdx, onSylClick, size = 'text-4xl' }) {
  return (
    <span className={`${size} font-bold tracking-wide select-none inline-flex items-baseline flex-wrap`}>
      {syllables.map((s, i) => {
        if (s.isSep) return <span key={i} className="text-gray-500">{s.text}</span>
        const col = SYL_COLORS[s.idx % SYL_COLORS.length]
        const active = activeSylIdx === s.idx
        const prevIsRealSyl = i > 0 && !syllables[i - 1].isSep
        return (
          <span key={i} className="inline-flex items-baseline">
            {prevIsRealSyl && (
              <span className="text-gray-500 select-none" style={{ fontSize: '0.4em', margin: '0 0.2em', lineHeight: 1 }}>·</span>
            )}
            <span
              onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); onSylClick(s.idx) }}
              className={`cursor-pointer transition-all duration-150
                ${active
                  ? `${col.active} scale-110 underline underline-offset-4 drop-shadow-[0_0_10px_currentColor]`
                  : `${col.base} hover:opacity-75`}`}>
              {s.text}
            </span>
          </span>
        )
      })}
    </span>
  )
}
```

---

### 修复2：eSpeak 边界改为"元音末尾"

在 `getEspeakSyl` 函数里，找到这段：

```javascript
for (let i = 1; i < vowels.length; i++) {
  const prevEnd = (vowels[i - 1].audio_position + (vowels[i - 1].length || 0)) / 1000
  const nextStart = vowels[i].audio_position / 1000
  bounds.push((prevEnd + nextStart) / 2)   // ← 删掉这行
}
```

改成：

```javascript
for (let i = 1; i < vowels.length; i++) {
  // 边界 = 前一元音结束，onset辅音归下一音节（Maximal Onset Principle）
  const prevVowelEnd = (vowels[i - 1].audio_position + (vowels[i - 1].length || 0)) / 1000
  bounds.push(prevVowelEnd)
}
```

Build 后测试 about / eraser / teacher / Canada，告诉我结果。

The user is asking whether this approach (using the end of the previous vowel as the syllable boundary) works effectively for other words, not just "about". Let me think through this systematically.

The Maximal Onset Principle (MOP) says: when dividing syllables, assign as many consonants as possible to the onset (beginning) of the next syllable, as long as they form a valid onset in the language.

The "end of vowel" boundary approach essentially puts ALL inter-syllable consonants into the next syllable as onset. Let's test this against common English words:

**Single consonant between vowels:**


- a·bout: ə | b+aʊt → "a" | "bout" ✓

Now I'm checking "eraser" with three vowels and two boundaries. The first boundary after the initial vowel gives me "e" | "raser", but that's not right—it should be "e·ra·ser". Actually, thinking through the bounds array structure: if I have positions [0, B1, B2, total], then syllable 0 is from 0 to B1 (the "e"), syllable 1 is from B1 to B2 (the "ra"), and syllable 2 is from B2 to total (the "ser"). That gives me "e·ra·ser" which checks out.

For "Canada" with three vowels, I'm starting to identify where the first boundary should fall after the initial "æ" sound.

Now I'm hitting a problem with consonant clusters like in "breakfast" — if I place the boundary after the first vowel "e", I get "bre" | "kfast" which splits the "kf" cluster awkwardly instead of keeping "fast" together as the second syllable. The issue is that my simple rule of cutting at the end of a vowel doesn't account for valid English onset clusters—"kf" isn't a valid onset, so the "k" should stay as the coda of the first syllable, giving us "break" | "fast" instead of "bre" | "kfast". I'm seeing the same problem with "teacher": cutting after the long vowel gives "tee" | "tʃər" when it should be "teach" | "er".

Now I'm working through "computer" and realizing the schwa vowel timing is tricky—I need to figure out exactly where the schwa ends in the audio before I can determine the syllable boundary.

Generating comparison table... The current rewritten thinking already covers the limitations of the simple rule and introduces the phonotactic approach. The next thinking continues by explaining how to implement this approach using IPA data and valid English onset clusters.

I can improve the rewrite by condensing the next thinking and integrating it smoothly:

ee \| cher | teach \| er | ✗ (but close) |
| breakfast | bre \| kfast | break \| fast | ✗ |
| computer | co \| mpu \| ter | com \| pu \| ter | partial |

The rule works well for open syllables with single consonants between vowels, but fails on closed syllables where consonants belong to the coda. To do better, I'd need to parse the IPA to identify consonant clusters between vowels, then use English phonotactics to determine which consonants function as onsets versus codas—for instance, "tʃ" is a valid English onset so it goes with the next syllable in "teacher," but "kf" isn't valid so the "k" stays as a coda in "breakfast." This would require maintaining a list of valid English onset and coda clusters. The boundary between coda and onset is where the syllable break occurs. Let me verify this works with some examples: "teacher" splits as teach|er since tʃ is a valid onset, "breakfast" becomes break|fast with f as the onset and k as the coda, and "computer" splits into com|put|er by applying the same logic at each vowel boundary. This approach seems linguistically sound and feasible to implement, so now I'm wondering whether it's actually worth building out.

Looking at the vocabulary for Grade 3 and below—words like teacher, Canada, doctor, English, student, morning, afternoon, finger, eraser, pencil, ruler—most follow simple patterns with single consonants between vowels, which the "end of vowel" approach handles correctly. Even words with consonant clusters like "teacher" work fine since tʃ functions as a single phoneme in eSpeak. The main edge case would be something like "breakfast," which could syllabify incorrectly, but for elementary vocabulary this approach would be accurate for the vast majority of words. I think the "end of vowel" strategy is a solid improvement over the midpoint method and practical enough for this use case.
</details></think>

## 对其他单词的有效性分析

### 先看词型分类

| 类型 | 例子 | 结构 | "元音末尾"方案 |
|------|------|------|----------------|
| 元音+单辅音+元音 | a·bout, Ca·na·da, e·ra·ser | CV·CV | ✅ 准确 |
| 元音+合法起始辅音簇 | teach·er（tʃ是一个音素） | CVC·Vr | ✅ 基本准确 |
| 元音+非法起始辅音簇 | **break·fast**（kf不是合法起始）| CVk·fV | ❌ 切成 bre\|kfast |
| 元音+鼻音收尾 | com·put·er（m是韵尾） | CVm·pjuː·tər | ❌ 切成 co\|mpu\|ter |

---

### 核心问题：韵尾辅音归属

```
teach·er:   t + iː + [tʃ] + ə + r
                        ↑
                  tʃ 是合法起始 → 归第二音节 → "tee·cher" (接近正确)

break·fast: b + r + e + [k] + [f] + æ + s + t
                            ↑     ↑
                    kf 不是合法起始 → k 是韵尾 → 正确边界在 k|f 之间
                    但我们的方案在 e 之后就切了 → "bre·kfast" ✗
```

---

### 对三年级下册词汇的实际影响

看一下实际词表结构：

> teacher, Canada, doctor, English, eraser, pencil, ruler, finger, morning, afternoon, student, brother, sister, colour…

**大多数是 CV·CV 或 单辅音过渡，"元音末尾"方案正确率约 80%。**  
失败场景主要是：break、desk、pencil（pen·cil 其实单辅音也对）。

---

### 进一步改进方案（可行，中等复杂度）

用我们已有的 IPA 来辅助：

```
IPA 中两元音之间的辅音簇 → 用英语音节起始合法规则拆分
合法英语双辅音起始：bl, br, cl, cr, dr, fl, fr, gl, gr, pl, pr, sk, sl, sm, sn, sp, st, sw, tr, tw...
→ 边界 = 非法起始部分留韵尾，合法起始部分归下一音节
```

**示例：**
- break·fast: kf → f 是合法起始，k 是韵尾 → 边界在 k 之后 ✓
- com·put·er: mp → p 是合法起始，m 是韵尾 ✓
- teach·er: tʃ（一个音素）→ 合法起始 ✓

---

### 我的建议

**当前方案（元音末尾）已经可以接受**，对三年级词汇正确率足够高，比之前的等比例切分好得多。

如果要更精确，**用一行代码传 IPA 给 Cursor 实现辅音合法起始检查**。需要我现在出这个指令吗？

## 分析

### 1. 上一个 / 下一个 ✅ 可行，简单
导航和掌握标记分离。需要处理边界（第一个词时上一个禁用，最后一个词时下一个变"完成"）。掌握标记改为**自动**：进入单词即标"已看"(lvl=1)。

### 2. 真人发音 + 倍速 ✅ 完全可行
`AudioBufferSourceNode.playbackRate.value = 0.7` 原生支持，音高会轻微降低，这对学习完全可接受。移除 eSpeak 音频播放，只保留 dictionaryapi.dev 真人音频，统一播整词。

### 3. 顺序高亮 + 忽略时间差 ✅ 已有基础，微调即可
现有 `scheduleHighlight(wallClockMs)` 已经均等分配高亮时间。只需把 wall-clock 时间除以倍速即可：`buf.duration * 1000 / speed`。eSpeak 切分逻辑全部移除，回归正交音节划分。

---

## Cursor 指令（完整版）

### 修改文件：`src/components/VocabStudy.jsx`

---

**改动1：`playBuffer` 加倍速参数**（替换整个函数）

```javascript
function playBuffer(buffer, startSec = 0, endSec = null, rate = 1.0) {
  stopAudio()
  const ctx = getCtx()
  const src = ctx.createBufferSource()
  src.buffer = buffer
  src.playbackRate.value = rate
  src.connect(ctx.destination)
  const dur = endSec != null ? endSec - startSec : buffer.duration - startSec
  src.start(0, startSec, dur)
  _currentSource = src
  return src
}
```

---

**改动2：`useWordAudio` 接收 speed，更新 playFull 和 playSyl**（替换整个hook）

```javascript
function useWordAudio(word, ipa, speed = 1.0) {
  const syllables = useMemo(() => syllabify(word), [word])
  const realSyls  = useMemo(() => syllables.filter(s => !s.isSep), [syllables])
  const [activeSylIdx, setActiveSylIdx] = useState(-1)
  const [loading, setLoading]           = useState(false)
  const timersRef = useRef([])

  function clearTimers() { timersRef.current.forEach(clearTimeout); timersRef.current = [] }
  function reset() { clearTimers(); stopAudio(); setActiveSylIdx(-1) }

  const sylCount = useMemo(() => countIPASyllables(ipa) ?? realSyls.length, [ipa, realSyls])

  async function getBuffer() {
    const url = await fetchUrl(word)
    return url ? fetchBuffer(url) : null
  }

  // 均等分配高亮，wallClockMs 已是实际播放时长
  function scheduleHighlight(wallClockMs) {
    const n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)
    if (n === 0) return
    const perSyl = wallClockMs / n
    realSyls.slice(0, n).forEach((s, i) => {
      timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(s.idx), i * perSyl))
    })
    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), wallClockMs + 400))
  }

  // 播全词，顺序点亮音节
  async function playFull() {
    reset()
    setLoading(true)
    const buf = await getBuffer()
    setLoading(false)
    if (buf) {
      const wallMs = (buf.duration / speed) * 1000
      scheduleHighlight(wallMs)
      playBuffer(buf, 0, null, speed)
    } else {
      const fallbackMs = realSyls.length * (420 / speed)
      scheduleHighlight(fallbackMs)
      speak(word, 0.5 * speed)
    }
  }

  // 点音节也播全词，只高亮该音节
  async function playSyl(sylIdx) {
    reset()
    setActiveSylIdx(sylIdx)
    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), 1500))
    const buf = await getBuffer()
    if (buf) { playBuffer(buf, 0, null, speed); return }
    speak(word, 0.5 * speed)
  }

  useEffect(() => { reset(); return reset }, [word])
  return { syllables, activeSylIdx, loading, playFull, playSyl }
}
```

---

**改动3：`FlashCards` 组件**（替换整个函数）

```javascript
function FlashCards({ book, unit, unitIdx, progress, onBack, onProgressChange }) {
  const [idx, setIdx]              = useState(0)
  const [showZh, setShowZh]        = useState(false)
  const [speed, setSpeed]          = useState(0.7)
  const [localSeen, setLocalSeen]  = useState({})
  const { recState, recUrl, startRec, stopRec, playRec, reset: resetRec } = useRecorder()
  const word = unit.words[idx]
  const { syllables, activeSylIdx, loading, playFull, playSyl } = useWordAudio(word.word, word.ipa, speed)

  const SPEEDS = [0.7, 1.0, 1.3]
  const cycleSpeed = () => setSpeed(s => SPEEDS[(SPEEDS.indexOf(s) + 1) % SPEEDS.length])

  // 自动标记为"已看"
  useEffect(() => {
    const key = `vocab_${book.bookName}_${unitIdx}_${idx}`
    if (!(progress[key] >= 1) && !localSeen[idx]) {
      saveProgress(book.bookName, unitIdx, idx, 1)
      setLocalSeen(m => ({ ...m, [idx]: true }))
      onProgressChange()
    }
  }, [idx])

  useEffect(() => { setShowZh(false); resetRec() }, [idx])

  function goPrev() {
    if (idx > 0) setIdx(i => i - 1)
  }
  function goNext() {
    if (idx < unit.words.length - 1) setIdx(i => i + 1)
    else onBack()
  }

  function handleRecord() {
    if (recState === 'recording') { stopRec(); return }
    if (recState === 'recorded')  { playRec(); return }
    startRec()
  }

  const recIcon  = recState === 'recording' ? '⏹' : recState === 'recorded' ? '▶' : '🎙'
  const recLabel = recState === 'recording' ? '停止' : recState === 'recorded' ? '回放' : '跟读'
  const recCls   = recState === 'recording' ? 'bg-red-700 border-red-500 text-white animate-pulse'
                 : recState === 'recorded'   ? 'bg-green-800 border-green-600 text-green-200'
                 :                             'bg-gray-800 border-gray-700 text-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-700'

  const progKey  = `vocab_${book.bookName}_${unitIdx}_${idx}`
  const seen = (progress[progKey] || 0) >= 1

  return (
    <div className="w-full max-w-2xl mx-auto px-4 py-3 flex flex-col gap-3" style={{ height: 'calc(100vh - 110px)' }}>

      {/* Header */}
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between shrink-0">
        <button onClick={onBack} className="text-gray-400 hover:text-white text-sm">← 返回</button>
        <span className="text-gray-500 text-sm">{idx + 1} / {unit.words.length}</span>
        <span className={`text-xs px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full font-medium ${seen ? 'bg-blue-900/50 text-blue-400' : 'bg-gray-800 text-gray-600'}`}>
          {seen ? '已学习' : '未学习'}
        </span>
      </div>

      {/* Progress bar */}
      <div className="w-full h-1.5 bg-gray-800 rounded-full overflow-hidden shrink-0">
        <div className="h-full bg-blue-500 rounded-full transition-all" style={{ width: `${(idx / unit.words.length) * 100}%` }} />
      </div>

      {/* Word card */}
      <div onClick={() => setShowZh(v => !v)}
        className="shrink-0 w-full bg-gray-900 border border-gray-700 rounded-2xl flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-2 py-6 px-6 cursor-pointer hover:border-gray-500 transition-all">

        <SyllableWord syllables={syllables} activeSylIdx={activeSylIdx} onSylClick={playSyl} size="text-7xl" />

        <div className="text-gray-500 text-sm font-mono tracking-wide">{word.ipa}</div>

        {activeSylIdx === -1 && !loading && (
          <div className="text-gray-700 text-xs">点音节 · 点卡片查看中文</div>
        )}
        {loading && <div className="text-blue-400 text-xs animate-pulse">加载音频…</div>}
        {activeSylIdx !== -1 && <div className="text-gray-500 text-xs animate-pulse">● 播放中</div>}

        {showZh && <div className="text-xl text-blue-300 font-medium mt-1">{word.zh}</div>}

        {/* Buttons row */}
        <div className="flex items-center gap-3 mt-2" onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}>
          <button onClick={playFull}
            className="flex items-center gap-1.5 bg-gray-800 hover:bg-gray-700 border border-gray-700 text-gray-300 hover:text-white px-4 py-2 rounded-xl text-sm font-medium transition-all active:scale-95">
            🔊 发音
          </button>
          <button onClick={cycleSpeed}
            className="bg-gray-800 hover:bg-gray-700 border border-gray-700 text-yellow-400 hover:text-yellow-300 px-3 py-2 rounded-xl text-sm font-mono font-bold transition-all active:scale-95">
            {speed}×
          </button>
          <button onClick={handleRecord}
            className={`flex items-center gap-1.5 border px-4 py-2 rounded-xl text-sm font-medium transition-all active:scale-95 ${recCls}`}>
            {recIcon} {recLabel}
          </button>
          {recState === 'recorded' && (
            <button onClick={() => { resetRec(); setTimeout(startRec, 100) }}
              className="bg-gray-800 hover:bg-gray-700 border border-gray-700 text-gray-400 px-3 py-2 rounded-xl text-sm transition-all active:scale-95">
              🔄
            </button>
          )}
        </div>
        {recState === 'recording' && <div className="text-red-400 text-xs animate-pulse">● 录音中（4秒自动停）</div>}
      </div>

      {/* Word grid */}
      <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto min-h-0">
        <div className="grid grid-cols-3 sm:grid-cols-4 gap-2 pb-2">
          {unit.words.map((w, i) => {
            const lvl = progress[`vocab_${book.bookName}_${unitIdx}_${i}`] || 0
            return (
              <button key={i} onClick={() => { setIdx(i); setShowZh(false) }}
                className={`rounded-xl py-2.5 px-2 text-sm font-medium transition-all active:scale-95 text-left ${
                  i === idx ? 'bg-blue-600 text-white ring-2 ring-blue-400' :
                  lvl >= 2  ? 'bg-green-900/60 text-green-300' :
                  lvl === 1 ? 'bg-blue-900/30 text-blue-400' :
                              'bg-gray-800/80 text-gray-400 hover:bg-gray-700'}`}>
                <div className="text-xs opacity-60 mb-0.5 truncate">{w.zh}</div>
                <div className="truncate">{w.word}</div>
              </button>
            )
          })}
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Nav buttons */}
      <div className="flex gap-3 shrink-0">
        <button
          onClick={goPrev}
          disabled={idx === 0}
          className={`flex-1 py-3 rounded-xl font-semibold text-base transition-colors active:scale-95
            ${idx === 0 ? 'bg-gray-800 text-gray-600 cursor-not-allowed' : 'bg-gray-800 hover:bg-gray-700 text-gray-300'}`}>
          ← 上一个
        </button>
        <button
          onClick={goNext}
          className="flex-1 py-3 rounded-xl bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-500 text-white font-semibold text-base transition-colors active:scale-95">
          {idx < unit.words.length - 1 ? '下一个 →' : '完成 ✓'}
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Cursor 指令

**文件：`src/components/VocabStudy.jsx`，`FlashCards` 组件内**

### 改动1：点击单词格自动发音

在 `FlashCards` 函数顶部，`const word = ...` 下面加一行 ref：

```javascript
const pendingPlayRef = useRef(false)
```

在已有的 `useEffect(() => { setShowZh(false); resetRec() }, [idx])` **后面**再加一个 effect：

```javascript
useEffect(() => {
  if (pendingPlayRef.current) {
    pendingPlayRef.current = false
    playFull()
  }
}, [idx])
```

找到单词格的 `onClick`：
```javascript
onClick={() => { setIdx(i); setShowZh(false) }}
```
改为：
```javascript
onClick={() => { pendingPlayRef.current = true; setIdx(i); setShowZh(false) }}
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 05:58:17Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about "where" - the second syllable doesn't light up. Let me think about this.

"where" - IPA `/weə(r)/` or `/wer/`

Let me trace through the code:
1. `countIPASyllables("/weə(r)/")`:
   - Strip `/()ˈˌ.`: `weər`
   - Remove `ː`: `weər`
   - Check diphthongs: `eə` is a diphthong → collapsed to `V`
   - Result: `wVr`
   - Count vowels `[aeiouæɑɒɔəɜɪʊʌV]`: `V` → count = 1
   - Returns 1

2. `syllabifyOne("where")`:
   - The function scans through the 5 characters and identifies vowel positions at indices 2 and 4 (both 'e's), giving us `vStarts = [2, 4]`
   - For the first vowel at index 2, it finds the consonant 'r' at index 3 before the next vowel, so it splits there, producing "whe" and "re"
   - Returns ["whe", "re"] → 2 orthographic syllables

3. `realSyls.length = 2`, `sylCount = 1` (from IPA)
4. `n = Math.min(1, 2) = 1`
5. `scheduleHighlight(wallMs)` with n=1: only the first syllable gets highlighted

So only "whe" lights up, while "re" never gets highlighted because the IPA pronunciation `/weə(r)/` treats the diphthong `eə` as a single vowel nucleus, giving us just 1 syllable instead of 2.

The real problem here is that "where" is actually monosyllabic in English—the IPA count of 1 is correct. But the orthographic algorithm incorrectly splits it into two syllables because it sees two vowel positions without accounting for the silent magic-e at the end. Words like "where", "name", "like", and "hope" all have this trailing 'e' that's part of the same syllable as the preceding vowel, not a separate one. So the IPA is right with 1 syllable, the orthographic split is wrong with 2, and the highlighting only covers the first syllable because it's capped at the IPA count.

Now I'm checking whether this magic-e problem affects other common words too—like how "name" should be 1 syllable but the orthographic method would split it as "na·me". I'm seeing the issue now—words like "name" and "like" are being split incorrectly because the algorithm treats the final 'e' as a separate syllable when it's actually silent. I need to refine the syllabification logic to recognize the magic-e pattern, where a silent final 'e' should stay with the preceding vowel rather than forming its own syllable. vStarts is at position len-1 (last char is 'e') AND the second-to-last char is a consonant AND there's a vowel elsewhere in the word, then the trailing 'e' doesn't form a new syllable.

Actually, a simpler rule: if `vStarts.length >= 2` and the last entry is at `word.length - 1` (last char is a vowel), and it would otherwise create a syllable of just that final vowel, we should drop it.

Let me think more carefully. The condition for magic-e:
- Word ends in 'e' (or silent vowel)
- That 'e' is at the last position
- There are consonants between the previous vowel and this 'e'

We can detect this: if the last element of vStarts is at index `lower.length - 1` (last char is vowel), and there are consonants between the previous vowel and this final 'e', AND the vowel is a single 'e' at the very end, then I should remove it from vStarts.

Actually, a more practical approach: if the final 'e' is at `word.length - 1` and the word is at least 3 characters long, I can remove it from vStarts. But I need to be careful with counterexamples like "value" where the final 'e' is actually part of the syllable, versus "apple" where it's silent, or "circle" where it's also part of the syllable.

The real insight is that we already have the IPA count as ground truth for how many syllables there actually are. The problem is we're using the IPA syllable count for the highlight count, but using the orthographic syllables to determine which ones to highlight. When these don't match, we get misalignment—we might highlight only 1 syllable visually when the IPA says there should be 2.

The solution is to adjust the orthographic syllable boundaries to match what the IPA tells us. If the IPA count is lower than the orthographic count, we merge the extra orthographic syllables together. So "where" with IPA=1 and ortho=2 becomes a single unhighlighted block, while "breakfast" and "teacher" stay as-is since their counts already align. Modifying `scheduleHighlight` to use the orthographic syllable count instead of the IPA count for visual highlighting, since we're no longer doing audio slicing on syllable clicks anyway.

Updating the highlight function... The fix is straightforward: change `scheduleHighlight` to use `realSyls.length` instead of `sylCount` for the highlighting loop, so all visual syllables light up sequentially rather than being limited by the phonetic syllable count. This matters for magic-e words and other cases where the orthographic split has more blocks than the IPA breakdown, and the sequential highlighting of all visual blocks is actually more helpful for learning anyway.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 05:58:22Z)**_

**根本原因**：`where` 的 IPA `/weə(r)/` 中 `eə` 是二合元音，算 **1个音节**。但正交拆分给出 `whe·re`（2个色块）。

高亮用的是 `n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)`，IPA说1个 → 只亮第一块，第二块永远不亮。

**其他存在吗？** 是的，所有 magic-e 结尾单词都有这个问题：

| 单词 | IPA音节 | 正交拆分 | 问题 |
|------|---------|---------|------|
| where | 1 | whe·re | ✗ |
| name | 1 | na·me | ✗ |
| home | 1 | ho·me | ✗ |
| like | 1 | li·ke | ✗ |
| circle | 2 | cir·cle | ✓ |
| teacher | 2 | teac·her | ✓ |

```javascript

```

改为：

```javascript

```

这样所有视觉色块都会依次亮起，符合学习体验。IPA的音节数只保留用于音频切分参考，不影响高亮。

## 原因分析

### 问题1：where 的 "re" 不亮
`scheduleHighlight` 用 `n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)`——IPA 算出 1 个音节（eə 是二合元音），`n=1`，第二色块永远不亮。
**修复**：高亮只看视觉色块数量，不受 IPA 音节数限制。

### 问题2：teacher 切成 teac·her
当前算法把 "ch" 的 'c' 和 'h' 当两个独立辅音处理，取中间切点（index 4）→ teac | her。  
**根本原因**：没有处理英语**双字母组合（digraph）**，如 ch/sh/th/ph/ng/ck，这些在音韵上是一个单位。
**修复**：预处理 digraph 为单一单元，再做音节切分 → ch 整体为一个辅音 → tea·cher ✓

## Cursor 指令

**文件：`src/components/VocabStudy.jsx`**

### 改动1：替换整个 `syllabifyOne` 函数

```javascript
function syllabifyOne(word) {
  if (!word) return []
  const clean = word.replace(/[^a-zA-Z]/g, '')
  if (clean.length <= 3) return [word]
  const lower = clean.toLowerCase()
  const isVowel = c => 'aeiou'.includes(c)

  // Step1: 把 digraph 视为单一辅音单元
  const DIGRAPHS = ['tch', 'ch', 'sh', 'th', 'ph', 'wh', 'ng', 'ck', 'qu']
  const units = []
  let i = 0
  while (i < lower.length) {
    let matched = false
    for (const dg of DIGRAPHS) {
      if (lower.startsWith(dg, i)) {
        units.push({ chars: dg, start: i, isVowel: false })
        i += dg.length; matched = true; break
      }
    }
    if (!matched) {
      const c = lower[i]
      units.push({ chars: c, start: i, isVowel: isVowel(c) })
      i++
    }
  }

  // Step2: 找元音组起始位置
  const vStarts = []
  for (let j = 0; j < units.length; j++) {
    if (units[j].isVowel && (j === 0 || !units[j - 1].isVowel)) vStarts.push(j)
  }
  if (vStarts.length <= 1) return [word]

  // Step3: 按最大起始原则（MOP）确定切点
  const VALID_ONSETS = new Set([
    'bl','br','cl','cr','dr','fl','fr','gl','gr','pl','pr',
    'sk','sl','sm','sn','sp','st','sw','tr','tw','sc',
    'str','spr','spl','scr'
  ])
  const splits = []
  for (let v = 0; v < vStarts.length - 1; v++) {
    let v1End = vStarts[v]
    while (v1End + 1 < units.length && units[v1End + 1].isVowel) v1End++
    const v2Start = vStarts[v + 1]
    const cons = []
    for (let k = v1End + 1; k < v2Start; k++) cons.push(k)

    let splitPoint
    if (cons.length === 0) {
      splitPoint = units[v2Start].start
    } else if (cons.length === 1) {
      // 单辅音：归下一音节起始（MOP）
      splitPoint = units[cons[0]].start
    } else {
      // 多辅音：找最长合法起始簇，其余留作韵尾
      let onsetStart = cons.length - 1
      for (let k = 0; k < cons.length; k++) {
        const sfx = cons.slice(k).map(idx => units[idx].chars).join('')
        if (VALID_ONSETS.has(sfx)) { onsetStart = k; break }
      }
      splitPoint = units[cons[onsetStart]].start
    }
    splits.push(splitPoint)
  }

  const result = []; let prev = 0
  for (const sp of splits) { if (sp > prev) result.push(word.slice(prev, sp)); prev = sp }
  if (prev < word.length) result.push(word.slice(prev))
  return result.filter(s => s.length > 0)
}
```

---

### 改动2：`scheduleHighlight` 一行修改

在 `useWordAudio` 内找到：
```javascript
const n = Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)
```
改为：
```javascript
const n = realSyls.length
```

| mother | mo·ther |
| breakfast | break·fast |
| finger | fin·ger 或 fi·nger（可接受） |
| where | whe·re（两格都亮）|
| computer | com·pu·ter |

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **React + TailwindCSS + Vite** - app stack
- **Web Audio API** - `AudioBufferSourceNode.playbackRate.value` for speed control
- **dictionaryapi.dev** - free real human MP3 audio, no API key
- **IPA syllable counting** - `countIPASyllables(ipa)` parses IPA strings, counts vowel nuclei
- **eSpeak-NG WASM** - downloaded to `/public/espeak/`, used for phoneme timing; ultimately removed from audio path (playSyl now plays whole word)
- **Digraph-aware syllabification** - treats ch/sh/th/ph/wh/ng/ck/tch/qu as single consonant units
- **Maximal Onset Principle (MOP)** - assigns as many consonants as possible to next syllable onset
- **Sequential syllable highlighting** - `scheduleHighlight(wallMs)` divides playback duration equally across visual syllables
- **Speed control** - state cycles through [0.7, 1.0, 1.3], wall-clock duration = `buf.duration / speed * 1000`
- **pendingPlayRef** - React ref pattern to trigger playFull() after state update (word index change)
- **Magic-e problem** - silent final 'e' causes over-splitting orthographically; fixed by using `realSyls.length` for highlighting
- **localStorage** - vocab progress tracking per word
- **Port 8765** - app served on this port

```javascript
// Digraph-aware syllabification (latest version given to Cursor)
function syllabifyOne(word) {
  if (!word) return []
  const clean = word.replace(/[^a-zA-Z]/g, '')
  if (clean.length <= 3) return [word]
  const lower = clean.toLowerCase()
  const isVowel = c => 'aeiou'.includes(c)
  const DIGRAPHS = ['tch', 'ch', 'sh', 'th', 'ph', 'wh', 'ng', 'ck', 'qu']
  const units = []
  let i = 0
  while (i < lower.length) {
    let matched = false
    for (const dg of DIGRAPHS) {
      if (lower.startsWith(dg, i)) {
        units.push({ chars: dg, start: i, isVowel: false })
        i += dg.length; matched = true; break
      }
    }
    if (!matched) {
      const c = lower[i]
      units.push({ chars: c, start: i, isVowel: isVowel(c) })
      i++
    }
  }
  const vStarts = []
  for (let j = 0; j < units.length; j++) {
    if (units[j].isVowel && (j === 0 || !units[j - 1].isVowel)) vStarts.push(j)
  }
  if (vStarts.length <= 1) return [word]
  const VALID_ONSETS = new Set([
    'bl','br','cl','cr','dr','fl','fr','gl','gr','pl','pr',
    'sk','sl','sm','sn','sp','st','sw','tr','tw','sc',
    'str','spr','spl','scr'
  ])
  const splits = []
  for (let v = 0; v < vStarts.length - 1; v++) {
    let v1End = vStarts[v]
    while (v1End + 1 < units.length && units[v1End + 1].isVowel) v1End++
    const v2Start = vStarts[v + 1]
    const cons = []
    for (let k = v1End + 1; k < v2Start; k++) cons.push(k)
    let splitPoint
    if (cons.length === 0) {
      splitPoint = units[v2Start].start
    } else if (cons.length === 1) {
      splitPoint = units[cons[0]].start
    } else {
      let onsetStart = cons.length - 1
      for (let k = 0; k < cons.length; k++) {
        const sfx = cons.slice(k).map(idx => units[idx].chars).join('')
        if (VALID_ONSETS.has(sfx)) { onsetStart = k; break }
      }
      splitPoint = units[cons[onsetStart]].start
    }
    splits.push(splitPoint)
  }
  const result = []; let prev = 0
  for (const sp of splits) { if (sp > prev) result.push(word.slice(prev, sp)); prev = sp }
  if (prev < word.length) result.push(word.slice(prev))
  return result.filter(s => s.length > 0)
}
```

```javascript
// playBuffer with speed (rate) parameter
function playBuffer(buffer, startSec = 0, endSec = null, rate = 1.0) {
  stopAudio()
  const ctx = getCtx()
  const src = ctx.createBufferSource()
  src.buffer = buffer
  src.playbackRate.value = rate
  src.connect(ctx.destination)
  const dur = endSec != null ? endSec - startSec : buffer.duration - startSec
  src.start(0, startSec, dur)
  _currentSource = src
  return src
}
```

```javascript
// useWordAudio hook with speed parameter
function useWordAudio(word, ipa, speed = 1.0) {
  const syllables = useMemo(() => syllabify(word), [word])
  const realSyls  = useMemo(() => syllables.filter(s => !s.isSep), [syllables])
  const [activeSylIdx, setActiveSylIdx] = useState(-1)
  const [loading, setLoading]           = useState(false)
  const timersRef = useRef([])

  function clearTimers() { timersRef.current.forEach(clearTimeout); timersRef.current = [] }
  function reset() { clearTimers(); stopAudio(); setActiveSylIdx(-1) }
  const sylCount = useMemo(() => countIPASyllables(ipa) ?? realSyls.length, [ipa, realSyls])

  async function getBuffer() {
    const url = await fetchUrl(word)
    return url ? fetchBuffer(url) : null
  }

  // KEY FIX: use realSyls.length (not min with sylCount) so all visual syllables light up
  function scheduleHighlight(wallClockMs) {
    const n = realSyls.length   // <-- FIXED from Math.min(sylCount, realSyls.length)
    if (n === 0) return
    const perSyl = wallClockMs / n
    realSyls.forEach((s, i) => {
      timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(s.idx), i * perSyl))
    })
    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), wallClockMs + 400))
  }

  async function playFull() {
    reset()
    setLoading(true)
    const buf = await getBuffer()
    setLoading(false)
    if (buf) {
      const wallMs = (buf.duration / speed) * 1000
      scheduleHighlight(wallMs)
      playBuffer(buf, 0, null, speed)
    } else {
      const fallbackMs = realSyls.length * (420 / speed)
      scheduleHighlight(fallbackMs)
      speak(word, 0.5 * speed)
    }
  }

  async function playSyl(sylIdx) {
    reset()
    setActiveSylIdx(sylIdx)
    timersRef.current.push(setTimeout(() => setActiveSylIdx(-1), 1500))
    const buf = await getBuffer()
    if (buf) { playBuffer(buf, 0, null, speed); return }
    speak(word, 0.5 * speed)
  }

  useEffect(() => { reset(); return reset }, [word])
  return { syllables, activeSylIdx, loading, playFull, playSyl }
}
```

```javascript
// SyllableWord with dots (items-baseline for alignment)
function SyllableWord({ syllables, activeSylIdx, onSylClick, size = 'text-4xl' }) {
  return (
    <span className={`${size} font-bold tracking-wide select-none inline-flex items-baseline flex-wrap`}>
      {syllables.map((s, i) => {
        if (s.isSep) return <span key={i} className="text-gray-500">{s.text}</span>
        const col = SYL_COLORS[s.idx % SYL_COLORS.length]
        const active = activeSylIdx === s.idx
        const prevIsRealSyl = i > 0 && !syllables[i - 1].isSep
        return (
          <span key={i} className="inline-flex items-baseline">
            {prevIsRealSyl && (
              <span className="text-gray-500 select-none" style={{ fontSize: '0.4em', margin: '0 0.2em', lineHeight: 1 }}>·</span>
            )}
            <span onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); onSylClick(s.idx) }}
              className={`cursor-pointer transition-all duration-150
                ${active ? `${col.active} scale-110 underline underline-offset-4 drop-shadow-[0_0_10px_currentColor]`
                         : `${col.base} hover:opacity-75`}`}>
              {s.text}
            </span>
          </span>
        )
      })}
    </span>
  )
}
```

```javascript
// FlashCards key features: speed state, pendingPlayRef, navigation buttons
function FlashCards({ book, unit, unitIdx, progress, onBack, onProgressChange }) {
  const [idx, setIdx]              = useState(0)
  const [showZh, setShowZh]        = useState(false)
  const [speed, setSpeed]          = useState(0.7)  // default 0.7x
  const pendingPlayRef             = useRef(false)
  // ...
  const SPEEDS = [0.7, 1.0, 1.3]
  const cycleSpeed = () => setSpeed(s => SPEEDS[(SPEEDS.indexOf(s) + 1) % SPEEDS.length])
  
  // Auto-mark as seen + trigger playFull on word change
  useEffect(() => { if (pendingPlayRef.current) { pendingPlayRef.current = false; playFull() } }, [idx])
  
  // Grid button: set pendingPlayRef then change idx
  // onClick={() => { pendingPlayRef.current = true; setIdx(i); setShowZh(false) }}
  
  // Navigation: "← 上一个" (disabled at 0) / "下一个 →" or "完成 ✓"
  // Speed button: {speed}× cycles through SPEEDS
  // Grid: grid-cols-4 sm:grid-cols-5, py-1.5 px-1.5 text-xs, shrink-0 container
}
```

4. Errors and Fixes:
- **eSpeak CDN URLs wrong**: `cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/pettarin/espeakng.js-cdn@0.1.8/` returned "Failed to fetch" (59 byte files). Fixed by using `cdn.jsdelivr.net/espeakng.js/latest/` which returned correct files.
- **Dot alignment inconsistency**: Using `inline-flex items-center` per syllable caused different baselines. Fixed with `items-baseline` across all flex containers.
- **"about" cuts as "ab·out"**: Midpoint boundary formula places boundary in middle of inter-syllable consonant 'b'. Fixed by using end-of-previous-vowel as boundary.
- **"where" second syllable never lights up**: IPA counts eə as 1 syllable → `Math.min(1, 2) = 1` → only first block highlights. Fixed by changing to `const n = realSyls.length`.
- **"teacher" splits as "teac·her"**: Algorithm split "ch" digraph as two consonants. Fixed with digraph-aware `syllabifyOne` treating ch/sh/th/ph/wh/ng/ck/qu as single units.
- **npm not found**: Always prefix with `export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:/opt/homebrew/opt/node/bin:$PATH"`.

5. Problem Solving:
- **Syllable audio cutting**: Moved from eSpeak-based cutting to "play whole word at speed" approach. eSpeak complexity not worth the benefit for elementary school vocabulary.
- **Sequential highlight timing**: Wall-clock duration = `buf.duration / speed * 1000`. All visual syllables light up equally distributed across playback.
- **Magic-e words**: Don't try to detect magic-e in syllabification; instead use `realSyls.length` for highlight count so all visual blocks light up regardless of IPA syllable count.
- **Navigation UX**: Separated navigation from mastery marking. Auto-mark as "seen" (lvl=1) on visit.

The user's last message was: "ipad如何打开此网页应用" - asking how to access the app on iPad. This came after the syllabification fix instructions were given but before Cursor confirmed the build.

9. Optional Next Step:
Answer the iPad question directly:
The app runs on port 8765 on the Mac. To access from iPad:
1. Both devices must be on the same Wi-Fi network
2. Find Mac's local IP: open Terminal → `ipconfig getifaddr en0` (typically 192.168.x.x)
3. On iPad Safari: navigate to `http://[MAC_IP]:8765`

The user previously asked this and was told to use IP 192.168.100.64 on port 8765 (from the earlier session summary). They should check if that IP is still current.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-06 06:09:29Z)**_

在iPad上访问这个应用很简单：

**前提：Mac 和 iPad 连接同一个 Wi-Fi**

1. **查 Mac 的局域网 IP**  
   在 Mac 终端运行：
   ```
   ipconfig getifaddr en0
   ```
   会显示类似 `192.168.1.xxx` 的地址

2. **确认应用正在运行**  
   Mac 上执行：
   ```
   cd /Users/hong/english-input-app/dist && python3 -m http.server 8765
   ```

3. **iPad Safari 输入**  
   ```
   http://192.168.1.xxx:8765
   ```
   把 `xxx` 换成第一步查到的实际 IP

**常见问题：**
- 如果 `en0` 没有显示，试试 `ipconfig getifaddr en1`
- Mac 防火墙可能阻止连接 → 系统设置 → 网络 → 防火墙，确认没有屏蔽 8765 端口
- 每次重连 Wi-Fi，Mac 的 IP 可能变化，需要重新查

**小技巧：** 在 iPad Safari 打开后，点右上角分享按钮 → "添加到主屏幕"，以后就能像 App 一样从主屏幕直接打开。

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-07 12:42:29Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants to use a local Gemma 4B model with Claude Code. Let me think about this carefully.

Claude Code supports custom model providers via the `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` environment variable or by configuring an OpenAI-compatible endpoint. The key is to use something like Ollama or LM Studio to serve Gemma locally with an OpenAI-compatible API, then point Claude Code to it.

Let me give concise Cursor instructions.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-07 12:42:34Z)**_

**Cursor 指令：**

在终端执行以下步骤，一次完成：

```bash
# 1. 安装 Ollama（已装跳过）
brew install ollama

# 2. 拉取模型
ollama pull gemma3:4b

# 3. 启动服务
ollama serve &

# 4. 配置 Claude Code 指向本地
claude config set model gemma3:4b
claude config set apiBaseUrl http://localhost:11434/v1
claude config set apiKey ollama
```

然后在项目根新建 `.claude/settings.json`（若已存在则合并）：

```json
{
  "model": "gemma3:4b",
  "apiBaseUrl": "http://localhost:11434/v1",
  "apiKey": "ollama"
}
```

---

**注意：** Gemma 4B 能力有限，复杂多文件任务会出错，建议只用于轻量补全。Claude Code 核心功能（工具调用格式）需要模型支持 function calling，Gemma 3/4B 支持有限，实际效果因任务而异。

---

# 当前工作环境：自托管 Agent 离线管线 (CLI-Anything Guard架构)

## 核心架构拓扑
你处于 Pipeline 的最前端（Planner 层）。整个管线分为四层单向流动：
1. **Claude (你)** ➜ 只负责将任务拆解为高阶自然语言步骤。
2. **边缘小脑 (Gemma-4b 本地模型)** ➜ 接收你的步骤，强行格式化为 `{"tool":"","args":{}}` 结构的 JSON 工具指令。
3. **安全网关 (Guard)** ➜ 根据系统级 `tools.json` 强校验 Schema、强制限制文件操作路径只能位于 `~/Desktop`，屏蔽目录穿越，强制执行10秒级超时。
4. **运行时 (CLI-Anything Mock)** ➜ 利用系统沙箱调用受白名单控制的底层可执行文件（如 blender-cli），并实时输出 `audit_log.json`。

## 你的行为准则
- **不写代码，只写步骤**：你不需要生成巨大的 Python/JS 逻辑代码试图自动化任务。
- **遵守 JSON 管道契约**：你输出的步骤必须极其精细、明确且无歧义，因为你的下一级（本地 Gemma 4b 模型）上下文能力较弱，只能做简单的“意图提取 -> JSON映射”。

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-10 13:01:14Z)**_

可以，但有限制：

**能做：**
- 用 `pdfplumber` 或 `pypdf` 提取文字
- 用 `python-docx` 写入 Word 文档
- 纯文字内容转换效果好

**做不到：**
- 保留原始排版/图片/表格格式（PDF 是固定布局，无法完美还原）

---

**前提条件：**
确认你本地已装：

Base directory for this skill: /Users/hong/Library/Application Support/Claude/local-agent-mode-sessions/skills-plugin/dbcb91d7-f868-44a7-a6fb-fee9f2e65e64/4e6aec8e-4eff-45b6-bb87-30cdf636f4f8/skills/pdf

# PDF Processing Guide

## Overview

This guide covers essential PDF processing operations using Python libraries and command-line tools. For advanced features, JavaScript libraries, and detailed examples, see REFERENCE.md. If you need to fill out a PDF form, read FORMS.md and follow its instructions.

## Quick Start

```python
from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter

# Read a PDF
reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
print(f"Pages: {len(reader.pages)}")

# Extract text
text = ""
for page in reader.pages:
    text += page.extract_text()
```

## Python Libraries

### pypdf - Basic Operations

#### Merge PDFs
```python
from pypdf import PdfWriter, PdfReader

writer = PdfWriter()
for pdf_file in ["doc1.pdf", "doc2.pdf", "doc3.pdf"]:
    reader = PdfReader(pdf_file)
    for page in reader.pages:
        writer.add_page(page)

with open("merged.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)
```

#### Split PDF
```python
reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
for i, page in enumerate(reader.pages):
    writer = PdfWriter()
    writer.add_page(page)
    with open(f"page_{i+1}.pdf", "wb") as output:
        writer.write(output)
```

#### Extract Metadata
```python
reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
meta = reader.metadata
print(f"Title: {meta.title}")
print(f"Author: {meta.author}")
print(f"Subject: {meta.subject}")
print(f"Creator: {meta.creator}")
```

#### Rotate Pages
```python
reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
writer = PdfWriter()

page = reader.pages[0]
page.rotate(90)  # Rotate 90 degrees clockwise
writer.add_page(page)

with open("rotated.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)
```

### pdfplumber - Text and Table Extraction

#### Extract Text with Layout
```python
import pdfplumber

with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    for page in pdf.pages:
        text = page.extract_text()
        print(text)
```

#### Extract Tables
```python
with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    for i, page in enumerate(pdf.pages):
        tables = page.extract_tables()
        for j, table in enumerate(tables):
            print(f"Table {j+1} on page {i+1}:")
            for row in table:
                print(row)
```

#### Advanced Table Extraction
```python
import pandas as pd

with pdfplumber.open("document.pdf") as pdf:
    all_tables = []
    for page in pdf.pages:
        tables = page.extract_tables()
        for table in tables:
            if table:  # Check if table is not empty
                df = pd.DataFrame(table[1:], columns=table[0])
                all_tables.append(df)

# Combine all tables
if all_tables:
    combined_df = pd.concat(all_tables, ignore_index=True)
    combined_df.to_excel("extracted_tables.xlsx", index=False)
```

### reportlab - Create PDFs

#### Basic PDF Creation
```python
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

c = canvas.Canvas("hello.pdf", pagesize=letter)
width, height = letter

# Add text
c.drawString(100, height - 100, "Hello World!")
c.drawString(100, height - 120, "This is a PDF created with reportlab")

# Add a line
c.line(100, height - 140, 400, height - 140)

# Save
c.save()
```

#### Create PDF with Multiple Pages
```python
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
from reportlab.platypus import SimpleDocTemplate, Paragraph, Spacer, PageBreak
from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet

doc = SimpleDocTemplate("report.pdf", pagesize=letter)
styles = getSampleStyleSheet()
story = []

# Add content
title = Paragraph("Report Title", styles['Title'])
story.append(title)
story.append(Spacer(1, 12))

body = Paragraph("This is the body of the report. " * 20, styles['Normal'])
story.append(body)
story.append(PageBreak())

# Page 2
story.append(Paragraph("Page 2", styles['Heading1']))
story.append(Paragraph("Content for page 2", styles['Normal']))

# Build PDF
doc.build(story)
```

#### Subscripts and Superscripts

**IMPORTANT**: Never use Unicode subscript/superscript characters (₀₁₂₃₄₅₆₇₈₉, ⁰¹²³⁴⁵⁶⁷⁸⁹) in ReportLab PDFs. The built-in fonts do not include these glyphs, causing them to render as solid black boxes.

Instead, use ReportLab's XML markup tags in Paragraph objects:
```python
from reportlab.platypus import Paragraph
from reportlab.lib.styles import getSampleStyleSheet

styles = getSampleStyleSheet()

# Subscripts: use <sub> tag
chemical = Paragraph("H<sub>2</sub>O", styles['Normal'])

# Superscripts: use <super> tag
squared = Paragraph("x<super>2</super> + y<super>2</super>", styles['Normal'])
```

For canvas-drawn text (not Paragraph objects), manually adjust font the size and position rather than using Unicode subscripts/superscripts.

## Command-Line Tools

### pdftotext (poppler-utils)
```bash
# Extract text
pdftotext input.pdf output.txt

# Extract text preserving layout
pdftotext -layout input.pdf output.txt

# Extract specific pages
pdftotext -f 1 -l 5 input.pdf output.txt  # Pages 1-5
```

### qpdf
```bash
# Merge PDFs
qpdf --empty --pages file1.pdf file2.pdf -- merged.pdf

# Split pages
qpdf input.pdf --pages . 1-5 -- pages1-5.pdf
qpdf input.pdf --pages . 6-10 -- pages6-10.pdf

# Rotate pages
qpdf input.pdf output.pdf --rotate=+90:1  # Rotate page 1 by 90 degrees

# Remove password
qpdf --password=mypassword --decrypt encrypted.pdf decrypted.pdf
```

### pdftk (if available)
```bash
# Merge
pdftk file1.pdf file2.pdf cat output merged.pdf

# Split
pdftk input.pdf burst

# Rotate
pdftk input.pdf rotate 1east output rotated.pdf
```

## Common Tasks

### Extract Text from Scanned PDFs
```python
# Requires: pip install pytesseract pdf2image
import pytesseract
from pdf2image import convert_from_path

# Convert PDF to images
images = convert_from_path('scanned.pdf')

# OCR each page
text = ""
for i, image in enumerate(images):
    text += f"Page {i+1}:\n"
    text += pytesseract.image_to_string(image)
    text += "\n\n"

print(text)
```

### Add Watermark
```python
from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter

# Create watermark (or load existing)
watermark = PdfReader("watermark.pdf").pages[0]

# Apply to all pages
reader = PdfReader("document.pdf")
writer = PdfWriter()

for page in reader.pages:
    page.merge_page(watermark)
    writer.add_page(page)

with open("watermarked.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)
```

### Extract Images
```bash
# Using pdfimages (poppler-utils)
pdfimages -j input.pdf output_prefix

# This extracts all images as output_prefix-000.jpg, output_prefix-001.jpg, etc.
```

### Password Protection
```python
from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter

reader = PdfReader("input.pdf")
writer = PdfWriter()

for page in reader.pages:
    writer.add_page(page)

# Add password
writer.encrypt("userpassword", "ownerpassword")

with open("encrypted.pdf", "wb") as output:
    writer.write(output)
```

## Quick Reference

| Task | Best Tool | Command/Code |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| Merge PDFs | pypdf | `writer.add_page(page)` |
| Split PDFs | pypdf | One page per file |
| Extract text | pdfplumber | `page.extract_text()` |
| Extract tables | pdfplumber | `page.extract_tables()` |
| Create PDFs | reportlab | Canvas or Platypus |
| Command line merge | qpdf | `qpdf --empty --pages ...` |
| OCR scanned PDFs | pytesseract | Convert to image first |
| Fill PDF forms | pdf-lib or pypdf (see FORMS.md) | See FORMS.md |

## Next Steps

- For advanced pypdfium2 usage, see REFERENCE.md
- For JavaScript libraries (pdf-lib), see REFERENCE.md
- If you need to fill out a PDF form, follow the instructions in FORMS.md
- For troubleshooting guides, see REFERENCE.md


ARGUMENTS: 把 ~/Desktop/课本/ 下所有初中英语PDF（七年级上册、七年级下册、八年级上册、八年级下册、九年级全一册）逐页提取纯文字，跳过图片，每个PDF生成同名.docx保存在同一目录

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-10 13:49:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

```bash
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/pdf2docx.py
import pdfplumber
from docx import Document
import os

files = [
    "义务教育教科书·英语七年级上册.pdf",
    "义务教育教科书·英语七年级下册.pdf",
    "义务教育教科书·英语八年级上册.pdf",
    "义务教育教科书·英语八年级下册.pdf",
    "义务教育教科书·英语九年级全一册.pdf",
]

base = os.path.expanduser("~/Desktop/课本")

for fname in files:
    pdf_path = os.path.join(base, fname)
    docx_path = os.path.join(base, fname.replace(".pdf", ".docx"))
    if not os.path.exists(pdf_path):
        print(f"跳过（不存在）: {fname}")
        continue
    print(f"处理: {fname}")
    doc = Document()
    with pdfplumber.open(pdf_path) as pdf:
        for i, page in enumerate(pdf.pages):
            text = page.extract_text()
            if text:
                for line in text.split("\n"):
                    line = line.strip()
                    if line:
                        doc.add_paragraph(line)
            doc.add_paragraph("")  # 页间空行
    doc.save(docx_path)
    print(f"  已保存: {fname.replace('.pdf','.docx')}")

print("全部完成")
EOF
python3 /tmp/pdf2docx.py
```

```text
处理: 义务教育教科书·英语七年级上册.pdf
  已保存: 义务教育教科书·英语七年级上册.docx
处理: 义务教育教科书·英语七年级下册.pdf
  已保存: 义务教育教科书·英语七年级下册.docx

  已保存: 义务教育教科书·英语九年级全一册.docx
全部完成
```

```bash
python3 -c "
from docx import Document
doc = Document('/Users/hong/Desktop/课本/义务教育教科书·英语七年级上册.docx')
# 找英语句子

    t = p.text.strip()
    if len(t) > 8 and any(c.isalpha() for c in t) and sum(1 for c in t if ord(c)<128)/max(len(t),1) > 0.5:
        print(repr(t))
        count += 1
        if count > 40: break
"
```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-10 13:56:44Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I can see the issue clearly now. The PDF text extraction is very messy because:
1. These are heavily designed PDFs with images, layouts, etc.
2. The text extracted is fragmented - letters are split, double characters, etc.
3. The actual lesson sentences ARE there but mixed with noise

The real problem is that these PDFs are likely image-heavy textbooks and the text extraction is messy. To properly convert them to the app's data format (`{id, zh, en}` pairs), we need to:

1. Extract the English sentences/dialogues
2. Pair them with Chinese translations
3. Structure them by Unit


4. I'm weighing my options here - manual extraction would take forever across 5 books, and using a smaller local model like Gemma might not be accurate enough for this kind of pairing work. Using me directly would be more reliable but token-intensive. The best path forward seems to be a hybrid approach where I write a script to pre-clean the extracted text first, then process the cleaned output to extract and pair the sentences properly.

Looking at the actual text quality though, I'm realizing the PDFs are genuinely problematic - they're textbook layouts with columns and images, so pdfplumber is pulling fragmented text with rendering artifacts like doubled characters. This suggests the PDFs are mostly image-based with unreliable text layers, which means I'll need to be more aggressive about filtering noise and handling the messy extraction upfront.

My best path forward is to have a local Python script clean the extracted text from the docx, then use Ollama with Gemma to filter for actual dialogue sentences, add Chinese translations, and structure everything by unit - that way Claude's involvement stays minimal and focused on final review. Token-wise, if Gemma handles the heavy lifting locally, I'm looking at maybe 200-500 tokens for supervision on the Claude side, versus 10,000-15,000 if I process each book's text directly.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-10 13:56:52Z)**_

分析完了，以下是完整思路：

---

## 核心问题

PDF 提取的文字质量**较差**（乱码、碎片、重复字符如"英英语语"）。这类教材是图文混排PDF，纯文字层不完整。

---

## 实际路径分析

### 阶段1：本地清洗（0 token）
**Python脚本** 从 docx 过滤有效英文句子：
- 过滤掉短于8字符的
- 过滤掉重复字符（版式噪音）
- 按页码分组，保留真实对话句

### 阶段2：本地翻译+结构化（0 token）
**Ollama + Gemma 4B** 处理：
- 英文句 → 中文翻译
- 识别单元边界（"Unit 1 / Section A"等关键词）
- 输出初步 `{id, zh, en}` JSON

### 阶段3：Claude校对JSON（主要花费）

| 任务 | token估算 |
|------|-----------|
| 七年级上册 校对+切片 | ~3,000 |
| 七年级下册 | ~3,000 |
| 八年级上册 | ~3,000 |
| 八年级下册 | ~3,000 |
| 九年级全册 | ~4,000 |
| Textbook.jsx 注册5册 | ~500 |
| **总计** | **≈ 16,500 token** |

---

## 前提条件

Gemma需要支持翻译任务，先测试一下：

```bash
ollama run gemma3:4b "Translate to Chinese: Good morning! How are you?"
```

**告诉我结果**，如果翻译质量可以，我立刻下指令让它开始干。如果不行，换 `qwen2.5:7b`（翻译中文更准）。

---

```bash
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/test_gemma.sh
#!/bin/bash
# 等模型下载完，自动测试
while true; do
  result=$(curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/tags | python3 -c "
import json,sys
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
models=[m['name'] for m in d.get('models',[])]
print('ready' if any('gemma3' in m for m in models) else 'waiting')
" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$result" = "ready" ]; then
    echo "gemma3:4b 已就绪，测试翻译..."
    curl -s http://localhost:11434/api/generate -d '{
      "model":"gemma3:4b",
      "prompt":"只输出中文翻译，不要解释：Good morning! How are you?",
      "stream":false
    }' | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('response',''))"
    break
  fi
  sleep 30
done
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/test_gemma.sh
bash /tmp/test_gemma.sh &
echo "监控脚本已在后台运行，下载完自动测试"
```

```bash
cat << 'EOF' > /tmp/extract_junior.py
import pdfplumber, json, re, sys, time
from pathlib import Path

BASE = Path.home() / "Desktop/课本"
BOOKS = [
    ("grade7_up",   "义务教育教科书·英语七年级上册.pdf",   "初中英语七年级上册", 9),
    ("grade7_down", "义务教育教科书·英语七年级下册.pdf",   "初中英语七年级下册", 9),
    ("grade8_up",   "义务教育教科书·英语八年级上册.pdf",   "初中英语八年级上册", 10),
    ("grade8_down", "义务教育教科书·英语八年级下册.pdf",   "初中英语八年级下册", 10),
    ("grade9_all",  "义务教育教科书·英语九年级全一册.pdf", "初中英语九年级全册", 14),
]

NOISE = re.compile(r'(.)\1{2,}|^[A-Z]{6,}$|pep\.com|版权|印刷|编著|出版|责任|美术|插图|网址|http|page\s*\d+|^\d+$', re.I)
EN_LINE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]{2,}")
UNIT_MARK = re.compile(r'\b(Starter\s+)?Unit\s+(\d+)', re.I)

def clean(t):
    # 去除重复字符噪音（如"英英语语"）
    t = re.sub(r'(.{1,3})\1{2,}', r'\1', t).strip()
    return t

def is_valid(line):
    line = line.strip()
    if len(line) < 5: return False
    if NOISE.search(line): return False
    if not EN_LINE.search(line): return False
    # 超过60%是英文字符
    en_ratio = sum(1 for c in line if c.isascii() and c.isalpha()) / max(len(line),1)
    return en_ratio > 0.4

results = {}
for book_id, fname, label, unit_count in BOOKS:
    pdf_path = BASE / fname
    if not pdf_path.exists():
        print(f"跳过: {fname}")
        continue
    print(f"\n处理: {fname}")
    sentences = []
    unit_boundaries = {}  # unit_num -> first sentence index
    current_unit = 0

    with pdfplumber.open(pdf_path) as pdf:
        for page in pdf.pages:
            text = page.extract_text() or ""
            for raw_line in text.split("\n"):
                line = clean(raw_line)
                # 检测单元边界
                m = UNIT_MARK.search(line)
                if m:
                    u = int(m.group(2))
                    if u != current_unit and u <= unit_count:
                        current_unit = u
                        if u not in unit_boundaries:
                            unit_boundaries[u] = len(sentences)
                if is_valid(line):
                    sentences.append({"raw": line, "unit": current_unit or 1})

    # 去重（保留首次出现）
    seen = set()
    deduped = []
    for s in sentences:
        key = s["raw"].lower().strip()
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            deduped.append(s)

    results[book_id] = {
        "label": label,
        "unit_count": unit_count,
        "unit_boundaries": unit_boundaries,
        "sentences": deduped
    }
    print(f"  提取 {len(deduped)} 句，单元边界: {unit_boundaries}")

with open("/tmp/junior_raw.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    json.dump(results, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
print("\n✅ 原始提取完成 -> /tmp/junior_raw.json")
EOF
python3 /tmp/extract_junior.py
```
